subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, ADB, Freedom House, RSF, Lowy, WorldRiskIndex) + international press; state media EXCLUDED on governance.
The world's single most disaster-exposed country (WorldRiskIndex #1), compounded by the deadly M7.3 Dec 2024 Port Vila earthquake on top of the 2023 twin cyclones.

Standout drags: extreme/correlated disaster exposure, a structurally impaired 'golden passport' revenue base (EU visa-free access revoked Dec 2024), and chronic-though-newly-curbed political churn; offsets are a broadly free, cohesive society.

economic.notes: A fragile, aid-reliant subsistence base with an impaired citizenship-revenue pillar and quake-rebuild deficits.

fiscal_state.notes: Deficit widening to ~5% of GDP in 2025 on quake rebuild plus falling citizenship revenue; donor grants cushion but the base is fragile. (IMF Art IV 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: The Reserve Bank of Vanuatu manages the vatu against an undisclosed basket - limited but real autonomy with thin instruments. (IMF; RBV)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The vatu is not a reserve currency; reserves are adequate but on a downward trajectory. (definitional; IMF 2025)

inflation.notes: Easing toward target after post-shock spikes, with small-economy import-price volatility. (IMF 2025)

banking_stability.notes: Commercial banks reportedly well-capitalized/liquid, but a small, partly-offshore system with thin supervisory data. (RBV; ADB)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A tiny, undiversified subsistence/copra/kava/tourism economy, structurally import-dependent and aid-reliant. (World Bank; IMF)

institutional.notes: A largely independent judiciary against land-deal corruption, with a stretched bureaucracy and free-ish press.

rule_of_law.notes: A largely independent judiciary has jailed officials, but land-deal corruption and abuse of office are serious. (Freedom House 2025; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: A functioning but capacity-thin statistics office reliant on donor support. (IMF; World Bank)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A small, stretched bureaucracy strained by serial government turnover and recurrent disaster response. (Freedom House 2025)

press_freedom.notes: A generally free press, but officials have occasionally threatened journalists over critical coverage. (Freedom House 2025)

political_social.notes: Chronic churn now curbed by reform, over a broadly free and cohesive kastom society.

political_stability.notes: Historically among the Pacific's most unstable, but the 2023/24 anti-floor-crossing reform has held (Napat survived a Dec 2025 no-confidence motion 36-12). (RFA; ConstitutionNet)

civil_liberties.notes: Broadly respected speech, assembly, and religion in a functioning democracy. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Not a major migration destination; the CBI gives easy citizenship-access but no notable mistreatment record. (inference; Freedom House 2025)

social_cohesion.notes: A largely cohesive ni-Vanuatu society with strong customary/kastom structures and localized land/inter-island tensions. (Freedom House 2025)

demographics.notes: Young and growing - a potential dividend but a jobs/services burden amid skilled out-migration. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: Non-aligned and hedging Australia against China, with AML scrutiny on the citizenship scheme.

alliance_reliability.notes: Non-aligned, playing Australia (top donor) against China (expanded police/grant ties) - pragmatic hedging carries some leverage. (Lowy Pacific Aid Map 2025)

conflict_involvement.notes: No military conflict; far from any war theatre. (inference; Lowy)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Not sanctioned, but the CBI scheme draws AML scrutiny (EU action; an FATF re-evaluation due 2026) - reputational/de-risking exposure. (EU Council 2024; FATF)

physical_practical.notes: The world's #1 disaster-risk profile, with weak health/infrastructure and near-zero catastrophe insurance.

climate.notes: Extreme and worsening cyclone exposure (Pam 2015, twin cyclones 2023) plus sea-level rise, with minimal adaptation capacity. (UNEP; WorldRiskIndex)

healthcare.notes: A weak system with a high NCD burden and thin specialist care, reliant on overseas referral. (WHO; World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: Poor roads/grid/water outside Port Vila, worsened by the Dec 2024 quake and the 2024 Air Vanuatu collapse. (IMF 2025; UNDP)

crime_safety.notes: Generally low violent crime, with petty theft and post-disaster strain. (inference; Freedom House 2025)

disaster_insurance.notes: The world's #1 disaster-risk country with negligible catastrophe-insurance penetration - existential exposure. (WorldRiskIndex; inference)

summary: The world's most disaster-exposed state, and the December 2024 M7.3 Port Vila earthquake - atop the 2023 twin cyclones and the 2024 Air Vanuatu collapse - exposed how a single shock cascades through revenue, infrastructure, and connectivity at once. Its fiscal model leans heavily on a now-impaired 'golden passport' scheme (EU visa-free access permanently revoked in December 2024, revenue down ~50% from peak, AML scrutiny ongoing), leaving a thin subsistence-and-tourism base propped up by Australian and Chinese aid. The chronic political churn that defined it for decades may finally be easing under the 2023/24 anti-floor-crossing constitutional reform, which has held through 2025. The skew is firmly negative: a broadly free, cohesive society cannot offset catastrophic and correlated physical risk plus a structurally fragile revenue base.
